topics.name: Arithmetic → Algebra Bridge
topics.keyIdea: Prealgebra builds the bridge from arithmetic (specific numbers) to algebra (general rules with variables).
topics.example: Arithmetic shows 3+5=8; algebra shows a+b=b+a for *any* a and b.

questions.text: If a+b=10 and a=6, what is b?
Name one thing algebra lets you do that arithmetic alone cannot.
questions.answer: Write general rules, use variables, etc.

topics.name: Number Line & Integer Types
topics.keyIdea: Numbers live on an infinite number line. Integers have no fractional part.
topics.example: −3 is left of 0 (negative); 5 is right of 0 (positive); 0 is neither.

questions.text: Name the integer one unit right of −4.

topics.name: Positive / Negative / Nonnegative / Nonpositive
topics.keyIdea: - **Positive** → right of 0 - **Negative** → left of 0 - **Nonnegative** → 0 or positive - **Nonpositive** → 0 or negative
topics.example: −2 is negative and nonpositive, but not nonnegative.

topics.name: Commutative Property of Addition
topics.keyIdea: Order doesn't matter: **a + b = b + a**

topics.name: Associative Property of Addition
topics.keyIdea: Grouping doesn't matter: **(a + b) + c = a + (b + c)**

topics.name: Adding Zero
topics.keyIdea: **a + 0 = a** — zero is the identity for addition.

topics.name: Any-Order Addition (Mental Math Strategy)
topics.keyIdea: Use commutative + associative properties to pair numbers that make easy sums.

topics.name: Summing Consecutive Integers
topics.keyIdea: Pair smallest with largest; each pair has the same sum.

questions.text: Eight pairs each sum to 31. What is the total?

topics.name: Commutative Property of Multiplication
topics.keyIdea: **a · b = b · a** (also written a×b or ab)

topics.name: Multiplying by 1
topics.keyIdea: **a · 1 = a** — one is the identity for multiplication.

questions.text: If n × 1 × n = 64 and n is positive, what is n?

topics.name: Order of Operations
topics.keyIdea: Parentheses → powers → multiply/divide (left to right) → add/subtract (left to right).

topics.name: Distributive Property (Multiplication over Addition)
topics.keyIdea: **a(b + c) = ab + ac** and **(b + c)a = ab + ac**

topics.name: Expanding vs Factoring
topics.keyIdea: - **Expand:** a(b + c) → ab + ac - **Factor:** ab + ac → a(b + c)

topics.name: Distributive Property (Longer Sums)
topics.keyIdea: Factor a common number from a sum of any length: ab + ac + ad = a(b + c + d).

topics.name: Multiplying by Zero
topics.keyIdea: **a · 0 = 0** for every number a. Multiplying by 0 "destroys" the number.

questions.text: If a × b × c = 0 and a, b, c are integers, what must be true?

topics.name: Definition of Negation
topics.keyIdea: The negation of a, written **−a**, is the number you add to a to get 0: **a + (−a) = 0**

topics.name: Negation of Zero and Negatives
topics.keyIdea: - **−0 = 0** - Negation of a negative is positive: **−(−a) = a**

topics.name: Multiplying by −1
topics.keyIdea: **−a = (−1) · a** — negating equals multiplying by −1.

topics.name: Sign Rules for Multiplication
topics.keyIdea: - negative × positive = **negative** - positive × negative = **negative** - negative × negative = **positive**

topics.name: Definition of Subtraction
topics.keyIdea: **a − b = a + (−b)** — subtracting means adding the opposite.

topics.name: Subtraction as Reverse of Addition
topics.keyIdea: If **a + b = c**, then **c − a = b** and **c − b = a**.

topics.name: Negation vs Subtraction (Warning)
topics.keyIdea: Same symbol (−), different operations!

questions.text: How many numbers does negation use?
How many numbers does subtraction use?
Is −3 a negation or a subtraction?

topics.name: Subtraction with Zero
topics.keyIdea: - **0 − a = −a** (subtracting from zero) - **a − a = 0** (self-subtraction) - **a − 0 = a** (subtracting zero)

topics.name: Subtraction with Negatives
topics.keyIdea: - **a − (−b) = a + b** (subtracting a negative) - **(−a) − b = −(a + b)** (subtracting from a negative) - **−(a − b) = b − a** (negation of subtraction)

topics.name: Subtraction is NOT Commutative
topics.keyIdea: **a − b ≠ b − a** in general. In fact, **b − a = −(a − b)**.

topics.name: Subtraction is NOT Associative
topics.keyIdea: **(a − b) − c ≠ a − (b − c)** in general. Cannot regroup freely!

questions.text: Are the results in #1 and #2 equal?

topics.name: Subtraction-to-Addition Strategy
topics.keyIdea: 1. Change all subtractions to additions 2. Rearrange using commutative & associative properties 3. (Optional) Convert back to subtractions

topics.name: Mental Math with Subtraction (Grouping)
topics.keyIdea: Pair numbers being added with numbers being subtracted to cancel.

topics.name: Distributive Property over Subtraction
topics.keyIdea: **a(b − c) = ab − ac** and **(b − c)a = ba − ca**

topics.name: Clever Use of Distributive Property
topics.keyIdea: Rewrite numbers near round values to use distribution.

topics.name: Section Review / Challenge
topics.keyIdea: Combine all subtraction rules: definition, zero rules, negative rules, grouping strategy, distributive property.
topics.example: Temperature drop: high 5°F, low −3°F → difference = 5 − (−3) = 8 degrees

questions.text: The high was 12° and the low was −5°. By how many degrees did the high exceed the low?

topics.name: Definition of Reciprocal
topics.keyIdea: For a ≠ 0, the reciprocal of a (written **1/a**) satisfies **a · (1/a) = 1**.

topics.name: Zero Has No Reciprocal
topics.keyIdea: **1/0 is undefined** because 0 × anything = 0, never 1.

topics.name: Reciprocal Properties
topics.keyIdea: - Reciprocal of 1 is 1 - **1/(1/a) = a** (reciprocal of reciprocal) - **1/(ab) = (1/a)(1/b)** (reciprocal of product) - **1/(−a) = −(1/a)** (reciprocal of negation)

questions.text: Rewrite 144 ÷ 12 as multiplication by a reciprocal.
What is 9 ÷ 12 as a fraction in simplest form?

topics.name: Division by Zero is Undefined
topics.keyIdea: Never divide by 0. Check denominator before dividing!

topics.name: Division with Negatives
topics.keyIdea: Same sign rules as multiplication:

topics.name: Division is NOT Commutative or Associative
topics.keyIdea: Cannot swap or regroup division freely. Use division-to-multiplication strategy instead.

topics.name: Division-to-Multiplication Strategy
topics.keyIdea: 1. Change divisions to multiplications by reciprocals 2. Rearrange using commutative & associative properties 3. (Optional) Convert back to division

topics.name: Cancel Common Factors
topics.keyIdea: **(ac) ÷ (bc) = a ÷ b** when b and c are nonzero.

topics.name: Division Distributes over Addition (by a number)
topics.keyIdea: **(a + b) ÷ c = (a ÷ c) + (b ÷ c)** when c ≠ 0

topics.name: Dividing by a Sum Does NOT Distribute
topics.keyIdea: **a ÷ (b + c) ≠ (a ÷ b) + (a ÷ c)** in general!

questions.text: Compute 100 ÷ (10 + 15) and compare to (100 ÷ 10) + (100 ÷ 15). Are they equal?

topics.name: The 9 Core Properties (Assumptions)
topics.keyIdea: All other arithmetic rules follow from these:

questions.text: Which property lets you compute 7 × 103 as 7×100 + 7×3?
Name one operation that is NOT commutative.

topics.name: Key Warnings
topics.keyIdea: - Subtraction: not commutative, not associative - Division: not commutative, not associative - Cannot divide by zero - Can divide a **sum by** a number, but cannot divide a number **by a sum**

topics.name: Powers & Exponent Notation
topics.keyIdea: aⁿ = a multiplied n times. Base = bottom, exponent = top.

topics.name: Squares & Perfect Squares
topics.keyIdea: n² = n·n. Perfect square = square of an integer.

topics.name: Order of Operations with Powers
topics.keyIdea: Powers after parentheses, before × and ÷.

topics.name: Square of Negation
topics.keyIdea: (−a)² = a²

topics.name: (−a)² vs −a²
topics.keyIdea: (−2)² = 4 but −2² = −4. Parentheses matter!

topics.name: Square of a Product
topics.keyIdea: (ab)² = a²b²

topics.name: Square of a Quotient
topics.keyIdea: (a/b)² = a²/b²

topics.name: Cubes & Higher Powers
topics.keyIdea: a³ = a·a·a. 2⁵ = 32.

topics.name: a¹ = a
topics.keyIdea: Any number to the 1st power is itself.

topics.name: Power of Product / Quotient
topics.keyIdea: (ab)ⁿ = aⁿbⁿ; (a/b)ⁿ = aⁿ/bⁿ

topics.name: 2³ vs 3² Warning
topics.keyIdea: Exponentiation is NOT commutative.

topics.name: Cube of Negation
topics.keyIdea: (−a)³ = −a³ (odd power keeps sign)

topics.name: Even/Odd Exponent Signs
topics.keyIdea: Negative base: even exp → +; odd exp → −

topics.name: Product of Powers
topics.keyIdea: aᵐ · aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ

topics.name: Quotient of Powers
topics.keyIdea: aᵐ ÷ aⁿ = aᵐ⁻ⁿ

topics.name: Power of a Power
topics.keyIdea: (aᵐ)ⁿ = aᵐⁿ

topics.name: Zero Exponent
topics.keyIdea: a⁰ = 1 for every number a.

topics.name: Why a⁰ = 1
topics.keyIdea: Pattern: divide by base each step down.

topics.name: Computing with a⁰
topics.keyIdea: Replace a⁰ with 1 anywhere.

topics.name: a⁻¹ = Reciprocal
topics.keyIdea: Negative exponent = flip the fraction.

topics.name: Negative Exp Rules Still Work
topics.keyIdea: aᵐ·aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ for all integers m,n.

topics.name: Exponent Laws Cheat Sheet
topics.keyIdea: Product: aᵐaⁿ=aᵐ⁺ⁿ; Quotient: aᵐ/aⁿ=aᵐ⁻ⁿ; Power: (aᵐ)ⁿ=aᵐⁿ; (ab)ⁿ=aⁿbⁿ; a⁰=1; a⁻ⁿ=1/aⁿ

topics.name: Multiples
topics.keyIdea: b is a multiple of a if b = ak for some integer k.

topics.name: Common Multiples
topics.keyIdea: A common multiple of a and b is a multiple of both.

questions.text: Name a common multiple of 2 and 5.

topics.name: Divisibility by 2
topics.keyIdea: Last digit even → divisible by 2.

topics.name: Divisibility by 5
topics.keyIdea: Last digit 0 or 5 → divisible by 5.

topics.name: Divisibility by 10
topics.keyIdea: Last digit 0 → divisible by 10.

topics.name: Divisibility by 3
topics.keyIdea: Sum of digits divisible by 3 → number is.

topics.name: Divisibility by 9
topics.keyIdea: Sum of digits divisible by 9 → number is.

topics.name: Prime Numbers
topics.keyIdea: Integer > 1 with exactly two divisors: 1 and itself.

topics.name: Composite Numbers
topics.keyIdea: Integer > 1 that is not prime.

topics.name: 2 is the Only Even Prime
topics.keyIdea: All other even numbers divisible by 2.

topics.name: Prime Factorization
topics.keyIdea: Every integer > 1 = product of primes (unique).

topics.name: Factor Trees
topics.keyIdea: Keep splitting composites until all primes.

topics.name: Least Common Multiple (LCM)
topics.keyIdea: Smallest positive common multiple of two numbers.

topics.name: LCM via Prime Factorization
topics.keyIdea: Take each prime to the highest power appearing.

topics.name: Divisors (Factors)
topics.keyIdea: d divides n if n = dk for integer k.

topics.name: Counting Divisors
topics.keyIdea: From prime factorization p^a × q^b → (a+1)(b+1) divisors.

topics.name: Greatest Common Divisor (GCD)
topics.keyIdea: Largest integer dividing both numbers.

topics.name: GCD via Prime Factorization
topics.keyIdea: Take each common prime to the lowest power.

topics.name: Relatively Prime
topics.keyIdea: GCD = 1 means no common prime factors.

topics.name: Number Theory Summary
topics.keyIdea: Multiples, divisibility rules, primes, LCM, GCD.

topics.name: What is a Fraction?
topics.keyIdea: a/b = a parts of size 1/b. b = denominator, a = numerator.

topics.name: Fractions on Number Line
topics.keyIdea: a/b is a point; proper if a<b, improper if a≥b.

topics.name: Equivalent Fractions
topics.keyIdea: Multiply/divide top and bottom by same nonzero number.

topics.name: Multiplying Fractions
topics.keyIdea: Multiply numerators; multiply denominators.

topics.name: Cancel Before Multiplying
topics.keyIdea: Cross-cancel common factors to simplify first.

topics.name: Dividing by a Fraction
topics.keyIdea: a/b ÷ c/d = a/b × d/c (multiply by reciprocal).

topics.name: Reciprocal
topics.keyIdea: Reciprocal of a/b is b/a.

topics.name: Fractions to Powers
topics.keyIdea: (a/b)ⁿ = aⁿ/bⁿ

topics.name: Simplest Form
topics.keyIdea: No common factors in numerator and denominator (GCD=1).

topics.name: Comparing Fractions
topics.keyIdea: Common denominator or cross-multiply.

topics.name: Adding Same Denominator
topics.keyIdea: Add numerators; keep denominator.

topics.name: Adding Different Denominators
topics.keyIdea: Find LCD, convert, then add.

topics.name: Subtracting Fractions
topics.keyIdea: Same rules as addition.

topics.name: Mixed Numbers
topics.keyIdea: Whole + fraction: 2 1/3 = 2 + 1/3 = 7/3

topics.name: Fractions Summary
topics.keyIdea: ×: multiply tops/bottoms; ÷: multiply by reciprocal; +/−: common denominator.

topics.name: Expressions vs Equations
topics.keyIdea: Expression: no = sign. Equation: has = sign.

topics.name: Evaluating Expressions
topics.keyIdea: Replace variable with number, then compute.

topics.name: Like Terms
topics.keyIdea: Same variable part → can combine.

topics.name: One-Step Equations (+/−)
topics.keyIdea: Use inverse operation to isolate x.

topics.name: One-Step Equations (×/÷)
topics.keyIdea: Divide/multiply both sides.

topics.name: Two-Step Equations
topics.keyIdea: Undo +/− first, then ×/÷.

topics.name: Variables on Both Sides
topics.keyIdea: Move all x terms to one side.

topics.name: Distributive Property in Equations
topics.keyIdea: Expand first if needed.

topics.name: Distribution on Both Sides
topics.keyIdea: Expand both sides, then solve.

topics.name: Factoring Before Solving
topics.keyIdea: Factor out a common term first.

topics.name: Word Problems → Equations
topics.keyIdea: Define variable, write equation, solve.
topics.example: Age: Jane is x, Bob is x+3

questions.text: Three times a number minus 7 is 20. Find it.

topics.name: Inequalities
topics.keyIdea: Same solving steps as equations, but flip sign when × or ÷ by negative.

questions.text: Is x=3 a solution to x>2?

topics.name: Equations Summary
topics.keyIdea: Isolate variable using inverse operations.

topics.name: Decimal Place Value
topics.keyIdea: Each place is power of 10.

questions.text: What digit is in hundredths place of 12.507?

topics.name: Adding/Subtracting Decimals
topics.keyIdea: Line up decimal points.

topics.name: Multiplying Decimals
topics.keyIdea: Multiply as integers, count total decimal places.

topics.name: Dividing Decimals
topics.keyIdea: Move decimal to make divisor whole.

topics.name: Rounding
topics.keyIdea: Look at next digit: ≥5 round up, <5 round down.

topics.name: Decimals Summary
topics.keyIdea: Place value, align decimals for +/−.

topics.name: Ratios
topics.keyIdea: a:b compares two quantities. Can simplify like fractions.

topics.name: Multi-way Ratios
topics.keyIdea: a:b:c can be scaled together.

topics.name: Proportions
topics.keyIdea: a/b = c/d → cross multiply: ad = bc

topics.name: Unit Conversions
topics.keyIdea: Multiply by conversion factor = 1.

questions.text: How many feet in 3 yards (3 ft/yd)?

topics.name: Speed
topics.keyIdea: Speed = distance ÷ time

topics.name: Other Rates
topics.keyIdea: Rate = quantity ÷ time (or per unit).

topics.name: Ratios Summary
topics.keyIdea: Simplify ratios; use cross-multiply for proportions.

topics.name: Percent of a Number
topics.keyIdea: x% of n = (x/100)·n

topics.name: Percent Word Problems
topics.keyIdea: Part = percent × whole.

topics.name: Percent Increase/Decrease
topics.keyIdea: New = original ± (percent × original)

topics.name: Percents Summary
topics.keyIdea: Percent = per hundred; convert to decimal to compute.

topics.name: Square Roots
topics.keyIdea: √a is the nonnegative number whose square is a.

topics.name: Perfect Square Roots
topics.keyIdea: √(n²) = |n| for perfect squares.

topics.name: Square Roots of Non-Squares
topics.keyIdea: √2, √3, etc. are irrational (not exact integers).

topics.name: Estimating Square Roots
topics.keyIdea: Find nearest perfect squares.

topics.name: Simplifying √
topics.keyIdea: √(a²b) = a√b for a,b positive.

topics.name: √a · √b = √(ab)
topics.keyIdea: Multiply under the root.

topics.name: Square Roots Summary
topics.keyIdea: √ is inverse of squaring; simplify by factoring perfect squares.
